message:
fix(api): gate all pub error enums with #[non_exhaustive] (br-frankenpandas-tne4)

Adds #[non_exhaustive] to every public error enum in the workspace
so that future variant additions stop being SemVer-major breaks per
Rust's semver policy (https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/semver.html).

Touched enums (one attribute each):
  crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:27        FrameError
  crates/fp-io/src/lib.rs:23           IoError
  crates/fp-expr/src/lib.rs:142        ExprError
  crates/fp-index/src/lib.rs:1267      IndexError
  crates/fp-conformance/src/lib.rs:2890 HarnessError

Why: the br-fd90 SQL backend epic is in flight and will add IoError
variants for PostgreSQL / MySQL / chunksize / coerce_float paths.
br-niwb (error-path conformance) will add ~20 variants across
IoError + FrameError + ExprError for pandas-category parity.
br-7cfm (Panics contract, closed) already promoted some panics
to Err variants. Without #[non_exhaustive], each would have
forced 0.2.0 breaking bump on downstream users.

With #[non_exhaustive] in place:
- Downstream match-statements on these enums MUST have a _ arm
  (compile error at first build post-update otherwise). This is
  the one-time semver friction.
- After today, adding new variants is a minor-version-friendly
  change.
- cargo-semver-checks (future br-4clx sibling CI gate) will
  correctly flag future exhaustive matches as source-breaking
  rather than semver breaking.

Tests under rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenpandas_cc):
  cargo test --workspace --all-targets
    → 23 suites · 3186 passed · 0 failed · 13 ignored
  cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
    → 0 warnings, exit 0

This is the simplest release-readiness slice in the tne4 bead's
fix-shape. The sibling work (also annotating pub config structs
+ cargo-semver-checks CI gate) stays open under tne4 for
follow-up — this commit ships the high-leverage part.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
